levels.label: Generic
levels.copy: The Positioning & Brand Differentiation pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Interesting
levels.copy: Some Positioning & Brand Differentiation practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Relevant
levels.copy: Core Positioning & Brand Differentiation practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Differentiated
levels.copy: Positioning & Brand Differentiation practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Category-Shaping
levels.copy: Positioning & Brand Differentiation is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.icp-and-trigger-clarity: Define one primary ICP using trigger, buying context, fit, and exclusion criteria.
recommendations.category-and-alternatives: Map the real alternatives and write one contrast that changes the buyer evaluation criteria.
recommendations.value-and-proof: Turn the core claim into a quantified, bounded outcome with relevant proof.
recommendations.message-consistency-and-learning: Audit homepage, pitch, product, and sales copy and establish a message learning loop.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
